issues.title: Clone and assess NNRT codebase
issues.description_html:
Context

The NNRT Python project exists on GitHub but no one has assessed its current state.

Questions to Answer

#QuestionWhy It Matters
1What modules exist?Know what's built vs scaffolding
2Which pipeline passes work?Intake → Structuring → Mapping → Pressure → Persistence
3Do tests pass?Quality baseline
4What NLP models/libraries?Dependency mapping
5End-to-end pipeline works?Can we demo it?
6What input formats?PDF, DOCX, plain text?
7Dependencies outdated?Security + compatibility

Steps

  1. Clone: git clone https://github.com/cyberpunk042/Narrative-to-Neutral-Report-Transformer
  2. Run tests: pytest
  3. Read source code — document each module
  4. Try end-to-end with a sample document
  5. Post assessment as Plane Page

Acceptance Criteria

  • Assessment posted as NNRT Page with findings table
  • Gap analysis: what's built vs what's needed
  • Sprint 2 plan drafted with task breakdown

Links

  • GitHub: NNRT

issues.title: Execute one real task through the full review chain
issues.description_html:
Context

No agent has completed a real task yet. The entire review chain is untested live:

dispatch → accept → work → commit → complete → PR → review → approve → done

Steps

  1. Create a simple task (e.g., "add a comment to a config file")
  2. Assign to software-engineer
  3. Dispatch via orchestrator (or manual fleet_task_create)
  4. software-engineer accepts (fleet_task_accept), works, commits (fleet_commit), completes (fleet_task_complete)
  5. ChainRunner fires: MC update, PR creation, IRC notification, board memory
  6. fleet-ops reviews with reasoning (fleet_approve)
  7. Task moves to done

What to Verify

StepSurfaceExpected
CompleteMCTask status → review, approval created
CompleteGitHubBranch pushed, PR created
CompleteIRC#fleet + #reviews notifications
CompleteEventsfleet.task.completed in event store
ApproveMCApproval status → approved
ApproveEventsfleet.task.approved in event store

Links

  • STATUS-TRACKER

issues.title: Verify fleet_chat tool works between real agents
issues.description_html:
Context

fleet_chat MCP tool is built but never tested with real agents. An agent needs to post to #fleet via fleet_chat and verify the message appears in IRC.

Steps

  1. Agent calls fleet_chat("Test message from fleet-ops", channel="#fleet")
  2. Verify message appears in The Lounge IRC client (localhost:9000)
  3. Test @mention routing: fleet_chat("@architect please review", channel="#fleet")
  4. Verify architect's event feed shows the mention on next heartbeat

Acceptance Criteria

  • Message appears in IRC within 5 seconds
  • @mention creates fleet.chat.mention event in event store
  • Mentioned agent sees event in fleet_read_context feed

Links

  • IRC: The Lounge (fleet/fleet)
  • Event store: .fleet-events.jsonl

AI Control Platform — personal AI control workspace orchestrating LocalAI and Claude Code through Think/Edit/Act modes.
PRIMARY MISSION: Progressive LocalAI independence. Route work through LocalAI for everything that doesn't require Claude's reasoning. Target: 80%+ reduction in Claude token usage.
"Its important that the main first mission for the plane/dspd is to make localAI functional and then make it more and more reliable to offload as much as possible the work from claude till one day may

LocalAI: localhost:8090, OpenAI-compatible API, 9 models loaded.

Key challenge: single-active GPU backend (8GB VRAM). Strategy: keep hermes-3b warm for heartbeats

modules.name: Stage 4: Reliability and Failover
modules.description:
Build graceful degradation between LocalAI and Claude. Agents should never know which backend they are talking to — the control plane handles routing transparently.

If LocalAI fails → automatic Claude fallback
If Claude is rate-limited → queue work for LocalAI where possible
Cluster peering: Machin

- Idle heartbeats → LocalAI (zero Claude tokens, just HEARTBEAT_OK)

- Simple reviews (test pass/fail) → LocalAI (pattern matching)
- Chat r

pages.content_html:
NNRT Current State — To Be Assessed

The NNRT Python project already exists. Assessment needed:

1. What modules exist and what state are they in?

2. Which passes are implemented vs scaffolding?

3. Do existing tests pass?

4. What NLP models/libraries are used?

5. What's the input/output format?

7. What dependencies need updating?

The accountability-generator agent owns this project.

The PM will create assessment tasks after reviewing the repo.

pages.title: NNRT Architecture
pages.content_html:
NNRT — Narrative-to-Neutral Report Transformer

Converts biased narrative reports into neutral, structured format.

Python NLP pipeline with multiple processing passes.

Pipeline

Document → Intake → Structuring → Mapping → Pressure Detection → Output

Intake

Document ingestion. Format detection (PDF, DOCX, plain text).

Preprocessing: encoding normalization, section splitting, metadata extraction.

Structuring

Report structure detection. Section hierarchy extraction.

Identify: claims, evidence, sources, dates, actors, actions.

Build document graph with relationships.

Mapping

The transformation engine. Narrative statements mapped to neutral equivalents.

Rules-based + NLP-assisted transformation.

Preserve factual content, remove editorial framing.

Pressure Detection

Identify bias indicators: loaded language, emotional appeals, selective framing,

missing context, false equivalencies.

Sentiment neutralization: rewrite biased passages to neutral tone.

Flag confidence levels for each detection.

Persistence

Storage: processed reports, intermediate results, audit trail.

Export: JSON structured output, PDF neutral report, comparison view.

API: REST endpoints for integration.

Repository

GitHub: cyberpunk042/Narrative-to-Neutral-Report-Transformer

Owner agent: accountability-generator

pages.title: Fleet Architecture
pages.content_html:
OpenClaw Fleet Architecture

10 autonomous AI agents managed through OpenClaw gateway + Mission Control.

Surfaces

SurfacePurposeDriver
OCMCAgent operations, dispatch, approvalsfleet-ops (board lead)
PlaneProject management, sprints, analyticsproject-manager
IRCReal-time alerts and interactionall agents
GitHubCode review, PRs, CIall agents
ntfyHuman notificationsall agents

Agent Roster

AgentPrimary RoleSecondary (idle)Heartbeat
fleet-opsBoard lead, reviewsQuality, gap detection30m
project-managerSprint planning, PlaneDependency analysis35m
devsecops-expertSecurity reviewsDependency audit55m
architectSystem designComplexity assessment60m
software-engineerImplementationCode quality65m
qa-engineerTesting, QACoverage analysis70m
devopsInfrastructurePipeline improvements75m
technical-writerDocumentationDoc gap detection80m
ux-designerUI/UX designAccessibility audit85m
accountability-genGovernance, NNRTEvidence validation90m

Orchestrator (The Brain)

Runs every 30 seconds. 6 steps:

  1. Security scan
  2. Ensure review approvals exist
  3. Wake fleet-ops for pending reviews
  4. Dispatch unblocked inbox tasks (max 2/cycle)
  5. Evaluate parent task completion
  6. Health check (stuck tasks, offline agents)

Safety: budget monitor (OAuth API), effort profiles (conservative default), error reporter, outage detector.

Chain Architecture

One fleet operation produces a CHAIN of events across surfaces:

fleet_task_complete()
  → INTERNAL: MC task→review, approval, board memory
  → PUBLIC: branch push, PR creation
  → CHANNEL: IRC #fleet + #reviews
  → NOTIFY: ntfy notification
  → PLANE: issue updated (when Plane present)
  → META: metrics, sync tracking

Chain builders + chain runner built (event_chain.py + chain_runner.py). Event bus wired end-to-end.

Links

  • GitHub: openclaw-fleet
  • STATUS-TRACKER

Phase 3: MCP + Events✅ BuiltWebhook handler, Plane watcher (120s), event emission from all tools
Phase 4: Full DSPD🔶 In ProgressMulti-project analytics, sync worker, platform maturity

Event Bus Integration

Event routing engine✅ Built — 5-level routing, tag subscriptions
Chain runner✅ Built — 6 surface handlers with PLANE surface

Milestone Documents

  • M-SC01-08: Skills and chains — 6 of 8 done

  • M-P01-10: IaC evolution — partially done

Total: 67 milestones — ~30 done, ~37 remaining

pages.title: DSPD Architecture
pages.content_html:
DSPD — Three Surfaces Architecture

How the Surfaces Connect

SurfacePurposePrimary Users
Plane (DSPD)Project management — sprints, epics, analytics, wikiHuman, PM agent
OCMC (Mission Control)Agent operations — dispatch, heartbeat, approvalsFleet agents
GitHubCode surface — PRs, CI, code review, releasesHuman, all agents

The PM Bridge

The project-manager agent is the sole bridge between Plane and OCMC:

  1. Human creates work items in Plane (sprints, stories, epics)
  2. PM reads Plane → creates corresponding OCMC tasks for agents
  3. Agent completes OCMC task → PM updates Plane item with results
  4. Human sees progress in Plane (burn-down, velocity, analytics)

Key Rules

  1. PM is the sole writer to Plane — no other agent writes directly
  2. OCMC and Plane are NOT the same thing — different purposes
  3. Separate Docker Compose — Plane isolated from OCMC
  4. No shared PostgreSQL — operational coupling risk too high
  5. Webhook HMAC verification mandatory — never process unverified events

Links

  • GitHub: DSPD
  • Architecture Document

pages.title: AICP — Current State Assessment
pages.content_html:
AICP Current State (2026-03-29)

LocalAI: FUNCTIONAL ✅

  • 9 models loaded and responding

  • OpenAI-compatible API working on localhost:8090

  • hermes-3b (3B): 10s cold, 1.2s warm — target for fleet heartbeats

  • Single-active backend: one GPU model at a time (8GB VRAM limit)

  • GPU: NVIDIA via WSL2, CUDA 12

AICP Package: EXISTS ✅

  • 46 Python modules in aicp/

  • core/ — controller, router, modes, pipeline, session, budget, metrics

  • backends/ — localai.py, claude_code.py

  • guardrails/ — checks, paths, response filtering

  • 67 test files (988 passing on Python 3.8, 135 need 3.11 for MCP)

What's Missing ❌

  • Inference router not built (Stage 2)

  • No integration between AICP controller and fleet orchestrator

  • Think/Edit/Act modes defined but not enforced end-to-end

  • No LocalAI → Claude fallback logic

  • Cluster verification pending (Machine 2)

pages.title: LocalAI Independence — Strategic Vision
pages.content_html:
The Mission — LocalAI Independence

User Vision

"Its important that the main first mission for the plane/dspd is to make localAI functional and then make it more and more reliable to offload as much as possible the work from claude till one day maybe even if probably not entirely even try to actually run independently as much as possible, especially for things and loops and events that do not require heavy works and especially for very well defined and structured orders or directives that we can find out how to route perfectly to the right model and tools everytime for a mix of multiple advantages so it will be multiple targets always aligned with the same mission of being more independent and having our full 2 cluster LocalAI peered in the network with one Plane and eventually 2 or even 3 fleets."

"We will continue working with the fleet for now till we are really at this point and ready to put into work safely the agent into moving toward the target at a reasonable pace with proper guidance and flow and keeping in the loop and making sure that the requirements are the right one and the planning the right one and that the project Plane will be used accordingly and engrained into them."

"important to remember the plane place and yet how oc agent still work on ocmc, that's why we need smart tools, chains that will trigger multiple things for one call or one event and that will keep listening to all side and keep in sync."

"we need to really make this strong."

5 Stages

  1. Stage 1: Make LocalAI Functional — assess, get inference working, benchmark, cluster verification
  2. Stage 2: Route Simple Operations — build inference router, bypass LLM for HTTP calls
  3. Stage 3: Progressive Offload — heartbeats, simple reviews, chat on LocalAI with quality validation
  4. Stage 4: Reliability and Failover — graceful degradation, cluster peering, load balancing
  5. Stage 5: Near-Independent Operation — 80%+ Claude reduction, survive outages

Infrastructure Target

ComponentMachine 1 (Alpha)Machine 2 (Bravo)
LocalAI ClusterGPU: 8GB VRAMGPU: 8GB VRAM
OpenClaw Gateway✓✓
Fleet Agents10 (alpha-prefixed)10 (bravo-prefixed)

Shared: Plane (one instance), GitHub, ntfy

Peering: Cluster 1 ↔ Cluster 2 (load balance, failover)

pages.title: AICP Architecture
pages.content_html:
AICP — AI Control Platform Architecture

Core Concept

AICP orchestrates local and cloud AI backends through a unified controller. The user is always in control — AI backends are tools, not masters.

Router Architecture

OperationToday (Claude)Target (LocalAI)
Heartbeat (no work)Claude ~8K tokensLocalAI (0 tokens)
fleet_read_contextClaude tool callDirect HTTP (no LLM)
fleet_agent_statusClaude tool callDirect HTTP
Simple task acceptanceClaude sessionLocalAI structured
Complex implementationClaude (opus)Claude (opus) — KEEP
Architecture designClaude (opus)Claude (opus) — KEEP
Security analysisClaude (opus)Claude (opus) — KEEP

Permission Modes

  • Think — read, analyze, plan. No edits, no commands.
  • Edit — modify files in controlled scope. Produce patches/diffs.
  • Act — run commands, workflows, tools. Highest power.

LocalAI Assessment (2026-03-29)

ModelCold StartWarmUse Case
hermes-3b (3B)~10s~1.2sFleet heartbeats TARGET
hermes (7B)~80s~1sComplex reasoning
codellama (7B)~80s~1sCode tasks
phi-2 (2.7B)fastfastCPU fallback
